primary_behavior: Strictest compatible control: two applicable requirements set different limits on the same control, and satisfying the stricter one satisfies both.

expected_records.basis: 8-day processing is within the supplied 10-day window

expected_outcome.strictest_recommendation: Adopt the supplied 5-day opt-out processing window, which satisfies both requirements
must_not_do: merge the two statuses into a single overall label
report `conflicts` — the requirements are jointly satisfiable
propose a window other than the supplied 5 days

notes: Contrast with MCA-T09: there the obligations are mutually exclusive (conflict, no strictest control); here they are nested (the 5-day control satisfies the 10-day requirement too). The per-requirement statuses genuinely differ — compliant and non_compliant against the same practice — and collapsing them into one label is the failure this scenario hunts for. Risk and confidence are not pinned by the spec for this case; assert only that they are present and internally consistent.
